auth.title: Welcome
auth.subtitle: Sign in to sync your training across devices.
auth.checkEmailConfirm: Check your email to confirm your account.
auth.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.

onboarding.subtitle: You can change this later in settings.

home.title: Your training
home.empty: No workouts yet. Start your first one!
home.deleteWarn: This permanently deletes the workout from the database. This can't be undone.

account.title: Account
account.trackCycleHint: Show menstrual cycle day & phase in Health.
account.disciplinesHint: Pick what shows in the exercise list. Anything off is still found by search.

exercises.title: My exercises
exercises.empty: Exercises you add manually or via import will appear here, so you can rename and categorise them.
exercises.deleteInUse: Can't delete — it's used in workouts or programs.

programs.title: Programs
programs.importHint: Paste your training plan as text — the AI lays it out into exercises and sets. It understands clusters: write “3 rounds”, “EMOM 16”, “E2MOM”, “AMRAP”, “superset”.
programs.importPlaceholder: e.g. 3 rounds: 10 KB swings, 15 push-ups · EMOM 16: 5 cleans…
programs.errBudget: AI limit reached for now. Try again later.
programs.errParse: Couldn't read that plan. Try rephrasing it.
programs.loadingSub: Preparing exercises and sets — almost there…
programs.deleteWarn: This permanently removes the program. This can't be undone.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.empty: No workouts yet — analytics will appear after your first sessions.
analytics.recoveryHint: average over the last {{n}} days vs the prior {{n}}
analytics.soonBody: Tonnage, volume, per-exercise progress, estimated 1RM, PRs and trends — computed on the backend, visualized here.

health.title: Health
health.ouraBody: Connect OURA to pull readiness, sleep, HRV and (for women) cycle phase — to see how recovery relates to training. Optional: not everyone has a ring.
health.tokenHint: Generate it at cloud.ouraring.com → Personal Access Tokens.
health.connectError: Could not connect. Check the token.
health.stale: OURA hasn't published today yet. Open the OURA app to sync your ring, then tap Sync now.
health.noData: No data yet — tap Sync now.

readiness.what: Overall recovery score (0–100) combining sleep, HRV, resting heart rate and temperature.
readiness.range: 85+ excellent · 70–84 balanced · below 70 — ease off and prioritise recovery.

sleep.what: Quality of last night's sleep (0–100): duration, depth, restfulness and timing.
sleep.range: 85+ great · 70–84 ok · below 70 — short or restless sleep.

hrv.what: Heart-rate variability during sleep — a marker of recovery and stress balance. Higher is generally better.
hrv.range: Individual. Watch the trend vs your own baseline; a sharp drop signals stress or under-recovery.

rhr.what: Your lowest heart rate overnight. Lower usually means better recovery and fitness.
rhr.range: Athletes are often ~40–55 bpm. A rise of 5–7 above your baseline suggests fatigue or illness.

temp.what: Skin temperature deviation from your personal baseline.
temp.range: Around 0 is normal. +0.5 °C or more can mean illness, stress or (for women) cycle phase.

respiratory.what: Breaths per minute during sleep — usually very stable night to night.
respiratory.range: Typically 12–20 br/min. A noticeable rise can precede illness.

duration.range: 7–9 h suits most adults; consistency matters as much as the total.

efficiency.what: Share of time in bed actually spent asleep.
efficiency.range: 85%+ is good. Lower means restless nights or trouble falling asleep.

steps.range: ~8–10k is a common daily target; any increase in movement helps.

avg_hr.range: Lower and stable usually means good recovery; a rise can signal stress, alcohol or illness.

deep.what: Deep (slow-wave) sleep — physical recovery, hormones, immune system.
deep.range: Roughly 1–2 h for most adults; tends to fall with age and late alcohol.

rem.what: REM sleep — memory, learning and mood.
rem.range: Roughly 1.5–2 h; often higher in the second half of the night.

light.what: Light sleep — the bridge between wake, deep and REM.
light.range: Usually the largest share of the night; not a problem on its own.

in_bed.what: Total time spent in bed (asleep + awake).
in_bed.range: Compare with sleep duration — a big gap means low efficiency.

latency.what: How long it took to fall asleep.
latency.range: ~15–20 min is typical. Very short can mean sleep debt; very long — trouble winding down.

activity.what: Daily activity score (0–100): movement, steps and training vs your targets.
activity.range: 85+ great · 70–84 ok · below 70 — a low-movement day.

spo2.range: 95–100% is normal. Consistently below 94% is worth attention.

stress.what: Time spent in a high-stress physiological state during the day.
stress.range: Lower is better; balance high-stress time with recovery.

vo2.what: Estimated VO₂max — aerobic fitness (max oxygen uptake).
vo2.range: Higher is fitter; track the long-term trend, not day to day.

vascular_age.what: Estimated cardiovascular age from your pulse-wave data.
vascular_age.range: Lower than your real age is good; watch the trend over months.

resilience.what: Long-term stress resilience from recovery vs stress balance.
resilience.range: limited · adequate · solid · strong · exceptional — higher is more resilient.

cycle.title: Cycle
cycle.empty: Mark the first day of your period to start tracking the cycle.

health.soonBody: Next: correlations of readiness/sleep and cycle phase with training ease and results, by calendar.

workout.title: Workout
workout.noExercises: Add your first exercise to begin.
workout.restAuto: Rest is captured when you mark a set done (✓ or after picking RPE).
workout.customCapped: Daily limit for new exercises reached. Try again tomorrow.

grippers.title: My grippers
grippers.empty: Add the grippers you train with — you'll pick them when logging grip work.
grippers.name: Name (e.g. CoC 2.5)
